mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending and economic growth, which drives corporate revenue forecasts upward and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 60-day return when both series grow, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning as manufacturers cut output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the hit rate drops below 40% when industrial production declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 325.0 indicates persistent price pressures that force equity investors to discount future cash flows at higher rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation observations, or the signal information coefficient turns negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative average 20-day return, or its predictive power vanishes across multiple market cycles with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and reduced construction activity, which drags on broader economic growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The housing signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns during recovery phases, or the average forward return is statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling retail sales indicates inventory buildup and weakening demand absorption, triggering immediate profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect loses explanatory power in a multivariate model over a 3-year rolling window.
